title: Jenks (Imperial)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jenks}} {{Nickname}} {{Conflicting|[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]|[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Jenks-EE1.png]] |name="Jenks" |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=She/her[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]] |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Dark Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|It doesn't matter what you do to ''me''. The ''Emperor'' has been told the location of this space city… You're all ''doomed''.|Jenks, after her capture|}} In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] infiltrated the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] base on the ancient [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] of [[Nespis VIII]]. Posing as a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] named '''Jenks''', the spy reported on the New Republic's presence aboard the [[City/Legends|city]] to the [[Transfer essence|reborn]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] but was later captured during an attempt to plant a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] aboard the New Republic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Using the information provided by Jenks, Palpatine [[Destruction of the Nespis VIII spaceport|destroyed the city]] with the [[Galaxy Gun]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], although the New Republic had been able to evacuate beforehand. ==Biography== ===Infiltrating the New Republic=== {{Quote|''Emperor!'' Our ''spies'' within the Rebel Alliance have just reported the location of the ''Rebel command base''… the derelict ''Nespis VIII'' space city, near the Cron Drift.''"
"Excellent''. Arm my galaxy weapon… ''Target'' the coordinates of the new Rebel base. There will be ''no'' more 'demonstrations.' It is time to ''end'' this war.|Executor Xecr Nist and Emperor Palpatine|}} [[File:JenksInfiltrates-EE1.png|thumb|left|190px|Jenks infiltrated the New Republic while posing as a technician.]] During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] operated as a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] amidst the [[Transfer essence|reborn]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Dark Empire|period of rule]] in his final [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which include the [[destruction of Da Soocha V]], to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. using his newest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[Galaxy Gun]], Palpatine [[Destruction of Da Soocha V|destroyed]] [[Pinnacle Base]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] headquarters located on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Da Soocha V]].[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]] The New Republic survivors then relocated to [[Nespis VIII]], a derelict [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] in the [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]],[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] making it their new base of operations. In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, in which Jenks appears, to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]]. the Imperial spy successfully passed a [[New Republic Security Force|New Republic security]] screening and infiltrated the Nespis VIII base, posing as a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] named "Jenks."{{FactFile|65|HOM 1-2|Homing Beacons}} During her stay there, Jenks discovered that the [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] tribe, which the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]] had [[Skirmish on Ossus|encountered]] on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]],[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] were descended from [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Jenks reported that information, along with the coordinates of Nespis VIII and its status as the New Republic base, to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. ===Rooted out=== {{Quote|''Master Solo!'' This man attached an ''Imperial homing beacon'' to the ''Falcon's'' navicomputer!''"
[Chewbacca incapacitates Jenks]
"Homing beacon'', huh? Which ''side'' are you on, guy? Don't answer. I already ''know''.|C-3PO and Han Solo, as Jenks is captured|}} Continuing with her subterfuge, Jenks attempted to plant a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] on the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] of the New Republic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Making his way aboard the vessel while it was undergoing maintenance, Jenks planted the device. However, her act of [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] was witnessed by the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], who were passing by. [[File:JenksCaptured-EE1.png|thumb|right|210px|Jenks is captured by Han Solo and Chewbacca.]] The droids intended to report on Jenks' subversion to the ship's [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Her cover put at risk, Jenks pulled out her concealed [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and threatened to destroy C-3PO's [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]] to prevent the droid from revealing that sensitive information. However, Solo himself arrived to witness the scene, and C-3PO informed him of Jenks' actions. The spy fired her weapon at Solo but narrowly missed the captain. The Imperial was then attacked by Solo's [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] co-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who jumped at her from a compartment above Jenks' head and subdued her. Jenks was taken into custody, and her status as a spy was made known to the [[New Republic Provisional Council|New Republic leaders]]. The homing beacon she had planted aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'' was also deactivated. However, Jenks boasted before her captors that she had already informed the Emperor about the New Republic's presence on the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[City/Legends|city]], proclaiming that the New Republic forces on Nespis VIII were doomed. Alerted to the New Republic's presence, the Emperor fired his Galaxy Gun at Nespis VIII. Although the weapon's first [[particle disintegrator warhead]] failed to detonate, allowing the New Republic time to evacuate, the second [[Missile/Legends|warhead]] [[Destruction of the Nespis VIII spaceport|destroyed Nespis VIII]]. Using Jenks' information about the Ysanna, Palpatine also dispatched [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Military Executor]] [[Xecr Nist]] to Ossus to kidnap members of the tribe, as Palpatine hoped to use their [[DNA/Legends|DNA]] to produce new clone bodies for himself, although that scheme was [[Mission to Vjun (Operation Shadow Hand)|foiled]] by Skywalker and his Jedi. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I think ''you'' need a little rewiring.|Jenks threatens C-3PO with a blaster|}} [[File:JenksDroids-EE1.png|thumb|left|300px|Jenks' cover was blown when she responded angrily to C-3PO discovering her planted homing beacon.]] Jenks possessed enough skills as a spy to infiltrate the New Republic's Nespis VIII base as a mere technician, despite anti-spy security screenings in place. In reality, Jenks was devoted to Emperor Palpatine, feeding him vital information and believing in the inevitability of his victory. During her brief stay with the New Republic, Jenks established some contact with Han Solo, as the latter knew her by her assumed name. When confronted by C-3PO, Jenks quickly lost her temper, pulling out her [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and threatening to damage the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], ultimately exposing her true identity as a spy. Jenks believed that her capture was irrelevant in the grand scheme of things, as the Emperor was already aware of the New Republic's presence on Nespis VIII. Jenks had light skin and close-cropped [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. ==Equipment== In order to maintain her cover during the infiltration of Nespis VIII, Jenks wore a technician's [[uniform]], complete with a [[Hat/Legends|cap]], and she carried a concealed blaster pistol. ==Behind the scenes== ===Character development=== {{Quote|An Imperial spy, who had infiltrated the New Republic's ranks as a technician named Jenks, attempted to attach a beacon to the ''Falcon''{{'s}} navicomputer.|"Jenks" is established as an alias in ''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 65|}} "Jenks" first appeared in the [[Empire's End 1|first issue]] of the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'', written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Jim Baikie]]. The first issue was published on [[October 24]], [[1995]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54211/star_wars_empires_end_1995_1|text=Star Wars: Empire's End (1995) #1}} Jenks also featured in the comic's 1995 [[Empire's End (audio drama)|audio drama]]. While ''Empire End 1'' never established whether "Jenks" was the spy's real name or an adopted pseudonym, [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 65|issue 65]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' established that "Jenks" was, in fact, merely an alias used by the spy. ===Inconsistencies=== {{Quote|OK, Jenks. How'd you get past the Alliance security screening?''"
"''I'll tell you nothing.''"
"''No? Maybe I should just turn you over to Chewie here. You ever seen what Wookiees do to traitors? Especially Imperial ones?|Han Solo questions the captive Jenks in the ''Empire's End'' audio drama|}} [[File:JenksShoots-EE1.png|thumb|right|300px|Unlike the comic ''Empire's End 1'' (pictured), the ''Empire's End'' audio drama describes Jenks as confronting Han Solo and the Jedi Empatojayos Brand.]] Compared to the comic, the ''Empire's End'' audio drama presents several inconsistencies regarding Jenks' confrontation with Han Solo. The encounter between Jenks and Solo in the audio drama begins with Solo arriving accompanied by the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] Jedi [[Empatojayos Brand]]. Instead of firing a single shot, Jenks fires multiple, and she succeeds in hitting Brand, but the Jedi survives due to his [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic body's]] backup systems. After the attack, Solo questions Jenks as to how she managed to infiltrate the New Republic. When Jenks refuses to answer, Solo threatens to give her over to Chewbacca, insinuating that the Wookiee will resort to more violent forms of interrogation. Jenks is then locked up in the ''Millennium Falcon'' {{'s}} storage bay. In the comic, Jenks is seen tied up in the briefing room of Nespis VIII. For the purposes of this article, the events are portrayed as they were originally presented in the comic book. ==Appearances== *[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]] *[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]] {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jenks (Imperial)}} *[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|65|HOM 1-2|Homing Beacons}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jenks (Imperialer)#legends |es=Jenks (Imperial) |pl=Jenks (imperialny) }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mechanics]] [[Category:Personnel of the Dark Empire]] [[Category:Spies]]
